title: Meet Meeting
summary: The discussion centered on the new Google Meet organizational unit established by Usman, focusing on its user interface designed for scheduling meetings through session IDs, SME names, and preferred times, along with sending calendar invites and notifications via Google Chat. Pablo highlighted an issue with meeting link generation that he intends to resolve, while both he and Leonardo noted the importance of shared calendars for visibility among team members. They provided an update on their project, emphasizing the need for final adjustments before a buy decision by the end of the week, and identified potential challenges such as time zone confusion and security vulnerabilities, suggesting Google authentication for enhanced functionality. Leonardo is preparing to test the meeting's webhook and create video documentation, while also discussing data reprocessing options using S3 to prevent data loss, which both agreed is crucial for managing the large number of sessions effectively.

action_items.text: Leonardo Lima will add the scheduled date to the meeting information once the email is confirmed.
Pablo Fernandez will start updating the documentation and instructions, leaving placeholders for URLs and other necessary information.
Leonardo Lima will test the meeting webhook to ensure it is called correctly during the next scheduled meeting.
Leonardo Lima will create a folder called Video Extracts for storing video assets temporarily.
Leonardo Lima will implement a command to process meetings from the stored raw webhook data on S3.

key_questions.text: What is the timeline for finalizing the organizational unit settings?
What will happen if the meeting webhook does not work correctly?

topics.text: Organizational unit settings and account creation

chapter_summaries.title: Google Meet Settings and Scheduling Updates
chapter_summaries.description: Leonardo Lima and Pablo Fernandez reviewed the new Google Meet settings created by Usman, which will apply to all meetings hosted by Leonardo. They discussed the development of a user interface that allows users to schedule meetings, input session details, and receive calendar invites. Pablo noted the need to ensure that meeting links are generated correctly.
topics: Organizational unit settings and account creation

chapter_summaries.title: Project Update and Development Discussion
chapter_summaries.description: Pablo Fernandez and Leonardo Lima reviewed the current status of their project, highlighting the urgency to finalize changes before the week's end. They discussed the importance of addressing time zone issues and the integration of session IDs and email functionalities. Leonardo mentioned potential security concerns and the need for Google authentication to enhance the system's reliability.

chapter_summaries.title: Testing Meeting Webhook and Asset Management
chapter_summaries.description: Leonardo Lima is setting up a test for the meeting's webhook and intends to create video assets for documentation purposes. He mentioned that the meeting will be stored in a specific folder and that he has plans for moving assets later. Pablo Fernandez highlighted the need for fallback options to prevent data loss, especially given the potential length of the sessions.
